wiki.summary: “Blackberry Marmalade” is intro track to Vince Staples' sixth studio album Cry Baby. The song is about the experience of being black in America. It highlights dealing with racism and the exploitation of black culture. An official music video was released to coincide with the song, but it was age-restricted on YouTube (meaning only accounts over 18 years of age or people with a verified ID can watch it). wiki.content: “Blackberry Marmalade” is intro track to Vince Staples' sixth studio album Cry Baby. The song is about the experience of being black in America. It highlights dealing with racism and the exploitation of black culture. An official music video was released to coincide with the song, but it was age-restricted on YouTube (meaning only accounts over 18 years of age or people with a verified ID can watch it). When this happened, Vince took to Twitter and made a post: “YouTube has age-restricted the Blackberry Marmalade video, so if you are over the age of 18, make sure to share it with the youth around you. Our children deserve the truth.” Additionally, instrumental-wise the song features the use of a guitar in some elements, and Vince himself stated about the discourse around this element in multiple Tweets, the first being. “The dummies hear a guitar and immediately start calling shit punk rock. / Rock, Punk, Post-Punk, Grunge, New Wave, Indie Rock, and so on and so forth are all different, but they all come from the Cracka tryna be James Brown. Read more on Last.fm. User-contributed text is available under the Creative Commons By-SA License; additional terms may apply.
